global.title: ADR-002 splits SwarmClient's 10 responsibilities into focused StampManager and ShareManager classes
global.content:
**ADR-002: Extract StampManager and ShareManager from SwarmClient**

**Status:** Accepted (2026-04-10)

**Context:** `SwarmClient` had grown to 1,035 lines with ~30 methods covering 10 responsibilities â€” data CRUD, feeds, stamps, pricing, sharing, profiles, ACT, compaction, and more. The class became hard to test and reason about.

**Decision:** Extract two focused classes:

- `StampManager` â€” stamp lifecycle and pricing.
- `ShareManager` â€” profiles, sharing, and key derivation.

`SwarmClient` keeps thin delegation methods pointing at these managers so existing call sites continue to work.

**Consequences:**
- Positive: `SwarmClient` refocuses on core CRUD + feeds + manifests and drops to 531 lines.
- Positive: `StampManager` and `ShareManager` become independently unit-testable.
- Negative: delegation wrappers add a layer of indirection that readers must follow.

ADR-003 (same date) complements this by adding optional DI parameters (e.g. `bee: Bee` on `SwarmClient`, `provider: EthereumProvider` on `wallet-signer`) so all three classes can be constructed with injected mocks and tested without global patching.
global.description: Accepted 2026-04-10: SwarmClient shrinks from 1035 to 531 lines of core CRUD/feeds/manifests; StampManager and ShareManager become independently testable behind thin delegation wrappers.
